name: assistants
context: Build persistent AI assistants with threads, messages, and tool use via the Assistants API
info: The Assistants API provides a stateful, multi-turn conversation framework with persistent threads, automatic context management, and built-in tools. Unlike raw chat completions (see [[openai/chat]]), assistants maintain conversation history server-side, support file uploads, and can use tools like Code Interpreter (execute Python in a sandbox), File Search (RAG over uploaded documents), and custom function calling (see [[openai/function-calling]]). The workflow is: create an assistant with instructions and tools, create a thread, add messages, create a run, and poll for completion. Assistants can be reused across threads and sessions.

sections.title: Create an assistant
sections.body: Call `client.beta.assistants.create()` with name, model (see [[openai/models]]), instructions (system prompt), and tools array. The assistant ID is reusable across threads. Update with `client.beta.assistants.update()`. List all with `client.beta.assistants.list()`. Delete with `client.beta.assistants.del()`.

sections.title: Threads and messages
sections.body: Create threads with `client.beta.threads.create()`. Add messages with `client.beta.threads.messages.create(threadId, { role, content })`. Threads persist server-side and manage context window automatically. Messages support text, images, and file attachments. List messages with `client.beta.threads.messages.list(threadId)` to read the conversation history.

sections.title: Runs
sections.body: Create a run with `client.beta.threads.runs.create(threadId, { assistant_id })` to execute the assistant against the thread. Poll `runs.retrieve()` until status is `completed`, `failed`, or `requires_action`. For tool calls, status becomes `requires_action` — process the calls and submit outputs with `runs.submitToolOutputs()`. Use `runs.stream()` for real-time streaming. See [[openai/function-calling]] for handling tool calls.

sections.title: Built-in tools
sections.body: Code Interpreter: sandboxed Python execution for data analysis, calculations, and chart generation. File Search: RAG over uploaded documents via vector stores — supports PDF, TXT, MD, DOCX, and more. Function Calling: define custom JSON Schema tools the assistant can invoke (see [[openai/function-calling]]). Multiple tools can be enabled simultaneously.

sections.title: File search setup
sections.body: Create a vector store, upload files to it, and attach the vector store to the assistant via `tool_resources.file_search.vector_store_ids`. The platform automatically chunks, embeds (see [[openai/embeddings]]), and indexes documents. Supports PDF, TXT, MD, DOCX, CSV, JSON, and more. Maximum 10,000 files per vector store.

instructions.description: System instructions defining the assistant's behavior and persona

tools.description: Tools the assistant can use: code_interpreter, file_search, or function definitions

threadId.description: Unique identifier for the conversation thread

runId.description: Unique identifier for the execution run
